query: I am lactose intolerant but really want a creamy alfredo. Make it work.
expected_behavior: Calls ingredient_substitution to swap dairy (cashew cream / coconut milk / lactose-free cream) and still delivers an alfredo-style dish.
final_answer:
Lactose-free creamy Alfredo, made safe:

Ingredients (6 servings)
- 2 tbsp olive oil
- 2 tbsp butter, preferably lactose-free butter
- 1 tbsp chopped garlic
- 4 tbsp flour
- 1 cup lactose-free whole milk
- 1 cup chicken broth
- 1 cup Parmesan cheese (Parmigiano Reggiano)
- 1/2 tsp salt
- 1/4 tsp black pepper
- 1 lb fettuccine or pasta of choice

Steps
1. Bring a large pot of salted water to a boil and cook the pasta according to package directions. Reserve 1/2 cup pasta water, then drain.
2. In a large saucepan over medium-low heat, melt the butter with the olive oil.
3. Add the garlic and cook for about 1 minute, stirring so it does not brown.
4. Stir in the flour and cook for 1 minute to form a roux.
5. Whisk in the lactose-free milk and chicken broth. Bring to a gentle simmer.
6. When the sauce thickens, stir in the Parmesan, salt, and black pepper.
7. Toss with the cooked pasta. If needed, loosen with a splash of reserved pasta water.
8. Serve immediately.

Safety note: this version is lactose-free, but Parmesan and butter can still contain trace lactose unless you choose certified lactose-free versions. If you want, I can also make it fully dairy-free/vegan.

Source URL: https://www.stldairycouncil.org/2025/07/Lactose-Free-Alfredo-Sauce-Recipe
